plan: - Electrical: Raspberry Pi 5, ESP32, Camera, PIR sensor, Mic, Speaker, LED eyes, 18650 batteries, TP4056 charger, voltage regulator, cooling fan, power switch, WiFi antenna. - Mechanical Structure: Screw holes, wire channels, ventilation, mounting points, removable access panels, base and legs. - Custom 3D Printed Parts: Head (L/R), upper/lower body shells, left/right wings, tail section, battery door, electronics mounting brackets. - AI/Software: Python, OpenCV, TensorFlow Lite for motion/human/face detection, Flask web server, mobile app for streaming and alerts. - Motion: Optional servo motors in wings for movement, head movement mechanism. - Power/Integration: Internal compartments for all components, main electronics (18x11x11 cm), battery (8x5x3 cm), head compartment for camera/LED/mic.

originalPrompt:
Act as an expert robotics engineer, mechanical designer, electronics engineer, and AI developer.

I want to build a full-scale "Eagle Security Robot" inspired by a realistic Bald Eagle. The robot should function as an intelligent surveillance and security system while maintaining the appearance of a real eagle.

PROJECT NAME:
Aegis Eagle – AI Security Robot

=====================
GENERAL SPECIFICATIONS
=====================

Overall height: 45 cm
Body length: 25 cm
Wingspan: 60 cm
3D printed body using PLA or PETG.
The entire body should be hollow and designed to house electronics.

=====================
3D MODEL REQUIREMENTS
=====================

Design the robot in separate printable parts:

1. Head (Left and Right halves)
2. Upper Body Shell
3. Lower Body Shell
4. Left Wing
5. Right Wing
6. Tail Section
7. Base and Legs
8. Battery Door
9. Electronics Mounting Brackets

Wall thickness:
3–4 mm.

Add:
- Screw holes
- Wire channels
- Ventilation holes
- Mounting points
- Removable access panels

=====================
INTERNAL COMPONENTS
=====================

Include compartments for:

1. Raspberry Pi 5
2. ESP32
3. Raspberry Pi Camera Module
4. PIR Motion Sensor
5. Microphone
6. Speaker
7. LED Eagle Eyes
8. Two 18650 batteries
9. TP4056 charging module
10. Voltage regulator
11. Cooling fan
12. Power switch
13. WiFi antenna

=====================
INTERNAL DIMENSIONS
=====================

Main electronics compartment:

Length: 18 cm
Width: 11 cm
Height: 11 cm

Battery compartment:

Length: 8 cm
Width: 5 cm
Height: 3 cm

Head compartment:

Camera mount
LED mount
Microphone mount

Wing compartments:

Space for optional servo motors.

=====================
ROBOT FEATURES
=====================

The eagle robot should be capable of:

- Motion detection
- Human detection
- Face recognition
- Live video streaming
- Mobile alerts
- Recording video
- Warning voice announcements
- Night vision LEDs
- Head movement
- Optional wing movement

=====================
AI FEATURES
=====================

Use:
- Python
- OpenCV
- TensorFlow Lite
- Flask Web Server
- Mobile App Dashboard

The robot should:

1. Detect intruders.
2. Send notifications.
3. Save recordings.
4. Stream camera feed.
5. Play warning messages.

=====================
BLUEPRINT REQUIREMENTS
=====================

Generate:

1. Orthographic views
   - Front
   - Side
   - Top
   - Bottom
   - Exploded view

2. Internal component layout.

3. Electronics wiring diagram.

4. Mechanical assembly diagram.

5. Dimensions of every component.

6. Bill of materials.

7. Estimated weight.

8. Estimated battery life.

9. STL-ready part breakdown.

10. Step-by-step assembly instructions.

=====================
DESIGN STYLE
=====================

Create a realistic bald eagle appearance with hidden electronics.

The robot should look like a decorative eagle statue during the day and become an intelligent security robot at night.

Generate engineering drawings and a complete manufacturing blueprint suitable for 3D printing and prototyping.

subSteps.title: Print all 3D mechanical components and mounts
Install M3 brass heat-set inserts into all designated mounting points
Mount eye LEDs, camera, PIR sensor, microphone, and speaker into their respective 3D printed mounts
Mount Raspberry Pi 5, ESP32, TP4056 module, voltage regulator, cooling fan, power switch, WiFi antenna, and audio amplifier to their dedicated 3D printed mounts
Attach all servo motors to their specific 3D printed mounts
Insert 18650 batteries into the battery tray
Connect 18650 batteries in series to the TP4056 charging module and to the power slide switch
Wire the power slide switch output to the buck-boost converter input
Connect the buck-boost converter output to the Raspberry Pi 5, ESP32, PIR sensor, microphone, cooling fan, audio amplifier module, and all servo motors
Connect the Raspberry Pi Camera Module 3 to the Raspberry Pi 5 MIPI CSI port
Wire Raspberry Pi 5 UART to ESP32 UART (TX/RX cross-connection)
Connect the cooling fan PWM input to Raspberry Pi 5 GPIO, and the PIR motion sensor output and microphone analog output to ESP32 GPIO pins
Connect eye LEDs and all servo motor PWM inputs to designated ESP32 GPIO pins
Wire Raspberry Pi 5 I2S output to the audio amplifier module input, and connect the amplifier output to the speaker
Verify power distribution and voltage levels from buck-boost converter to all components using a multimeter
Flash ESP32 with initial firmware for GPIO control and communication with RPi5, and test eye LED functionality
Install Raspberry Pi OS on RPi5, enable camera, I2S, and UART interfaces, and test camera video feed
Develop and test basic Python scripts on RPi5 to communicate with ESP32 via UART, and control the cooling fan
Test PIR motion sensor and microphone input on ESP32, and verify data transfer to RPi5
Test audio playback through RPi5 I2S to audio amplifier and speaker
Develop and test basic servo control code on ESP32 for head and wing movements, ensuring correct PWM signals and range of motion
Mount the Raspberry Pi 5 assembly and ESP32 assembly into the lower body shell
Mount the battery tray, TP4056 module, voltage regulator module, audio amplifier, and power switch assemblies into the lower body shell, connecting the battery door
Assemble the head by joining the left and right halves, securing eye LED, camera, microphone, and head servo mounts
Mount the PIR sensor, cooling fan, and WiFi antenna assemblies into the upper body shell
Attach the head assembly to the upper body shell, ensuring proper routing of camera and servo cables
Assemble the left and right wings by mounting the inner and outer servo mechanisms to the wing parts, then attach the complete wings to the upper body shell
Secure the tail section to the upper body shell
Join the upper and lower body shells, route and secure all internal wiring, and finally attach the base and legs assembly

projectDescription: The 45cm 3D-printed Aegis Eagle is an intelligent surveillance robot disguised as a bald eagle. Powered by a Raspberry Pi 5 and ESP32, it uses a camera, PIR sensor, and speaker for motion detection, face recognition, live streaming, and issuing warning announcements, with optional head and wing movement.

imagePromptSnapshot.description:
Project idea: Act as an expert robotics engineer, mechanical designer, electronics engineer, and AI developer.

I want to build a full-scale "Eagle Security Robot" inspired by a realistic Bald Eagle. The robot should function as an intelligent surveillance and security system while maintaining the appearance of a real eagle.

PROJECT NAME:
Aegis Eagle – AI Security Robot

=====================
GENERAL SPECIFICATIONS
=====================

Overall height: 45 cm
Body length: 25 cm
Wingspan: 60 cm
3D printed body using PLA or PETG.
The entire body should be hollow and designed to house electronics.

=====================
3D MODEL REQUIREMENTS
=====================

Design the robot in separate printable parts:

1. Head (Left and Right halves)
2. Upper Body Shell
3. Lower Body Shell
4. Left Wing
5. Right Wing
6. Tail Section
7. Base and Legs
8. Battery Door
9. Electronics Mounting Brackets

Wall thickness:
3–4 mm.

Add:
- Screw holes
- Wire channels
- Ventilation holes
- Mounting points
- Removable access panels

=====================
INTERNAL COMPONENTS
=====================

Include compartments for:

1. Raspberry Pi 5
2. ESP32
3. Raspberry Pi Camera Module
4. PIR Motion Sensor
5. Microphone
6. Speaker
7. LED Eagle Eyes
8. Two 18650 batteries
9. TP4056 charging module
10. Voltage regulator
11. Cooling fan
12. Power switch
13. WiFi antenna

=====================
INTERNAL DIMENSIONS
=====================

Main electronics compartment:

Length: 18 cm
Width: 11 cm
Height: 11 cm

Battery compartment:

Length: 8 cm
Width: 5 cm
Height: 3 cm

Head compartment:

Camera mount
LED mount
Microphone mount

Wing compartments:

Space for optional servo motors.

=====================
ROBOT FEATURES
=====================

The eagle robot should be capable of:

- Motion detection
- Human detection
- Face recognition
- Live video streaming
- Mobile alerts
- Recording video
- Warning voice announcements
- Night vision LEDs
- Head movement
- Optional wing movement

=====================
AI FEATURES
=====================

Use:
- Python
- OpenCV
- TensorFlow Lite
- Flask Web Server
- Mobile App Dashboard

The robot should:

1. Detect intruders.
2. Send notifications.
3. Save recordings.
4. Stream camera feed.
5. Play warning messages.

=====================
BLUEPRINT REQUIREMENTS
=====================

Generate:

1. Orthographic views
   - Front
   - Side
   - Top
   - Bottom
   - Exploded view

2. Internal component layout.

3. Electronics wiring diagram.

4. Mechanical assembly diagram.

5. Dimensions of every component.

6. Bill of materials.

7. Estimated weight.

8. Estimated battery life.

9. STL-ready part breakdown.

10. Step-by-step assembly instructions.

=====================
DESIGN STYLE
=====================

Create a realistic bald eagle appearance with hidden electronics.

The robot should look like a decorative eagle statue during the day and become an intelligent security robot at night.

Generate engineering drawings and a complete manufacturing blueprint suitable for 3D printing and prototyping.

Design notes: Realistic bald eagle appearance, Intelligent surveillance system, 3D printed PLA/PETG, Hidden electronics design, Hollow body for components, 45 cm overall height

Electrical components (current): 2x mcu: Main AI Controller, Secondary Control MCU; 3x sensor: Main Vision Camera, Body Motion Detector, Audio Input Mic; 9x actuator: Warning Announcement Speaker, Right Eye LED, Left Eye LED, Electronics Cooling Fan +5 more; 4x power: Main Power Cell 1, Main Power Cell 2, Battery Charging Module, Voltage Regulator Module; 3x module: Main Power Switch, External WiFi Antenna, Speaker Amplifier

Mechanical/structural parts (current): 29x 3d_printed: Head Left Half, Head Right Half, Upper Body Shell, Lower Body Shell +25 more; 2x misc: M3 Brass Heat-Set Inserts, M3 Pan Head Screws; 5x mechanism: Head Movement Servo, Left Wing Inner Joint Servo, Right Wing Inner Joint Servo, Left Wing Outer Joint Servo +1 more

Assembly: Head Left Half → Head Right Half (M3 screws with heat-set inserts); Head Left Half → Left Eye LED Mount (snap fit or M3 screws); Head Right Half → Right Eye LED Mount (snap fit or M3 screws); Upper Body Shell → Lower Body Shell (M3 screws with heat-set inserts); Upper Body Shell → Head Left Half (M3 screws with heat-set inserts (via head servo mechanism)); Upper Body Shell → Head Right Half (M3 screws with heat-set inserts (via head servo mechanism)); Upper Body Shell → Left Wing (M3 screws with heat-set inserts (via wing servo mechanisms)); Upper Body Shell → Right Wing (M3 screws with heat-set inserts (via wing servo mechanisms)); Upper Body Shell → Tail Section (M3 screws with heat-set inserts); Lower Body Shell → Base and Legs (M3 screws with heat-set inserts); Lower Body Shell → Battery Compartment Door (Hinged with M3 screws or snap fit); Raspberry Pi 5 Mount → Lower Body Shell (M3 screws with heat-set inserts); ESP32 Mount → Lower Body Shell (M3 screws with heat-set inserts); RPi Camera Module Mount → Head Left Half (M3 screws); PIR Sensor Mount → Upper Body Shell (M3 screws) (+46 more connections)

tools: Hot glue gun (optional, for securing wires)
assumptions: Understanding of basic electronics wiring and safety
Access to a computer with Raspberry Pi OS/Linux environment for software development
Arduino IDE or PlatformIO installed for ESP32 programming
Appropriate small gauge wiring for electrical connections
Knowledge of GPIO pinouts for Raspberry Pi 5 and ESP32
